items.title: Shakespeare, Bowie, Einstein: Mona opens one-of-a-kind underground library

children.content: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion,
is anything but ordinary.

gallery.altText: Book on display with hand reaching for a dial device.
Room with curved seating and roof design.
Shelves filled with books line the walls of a library. At the end of the corridor is a cave-like reading room.

items.title: Bird flu could be catastrophic for nation's vulnerable species, experts warn

children.content: The black swan, the Tasmanian devil and Australian sea lions are among the species at risk if a deadly strain of avian influenza takes hold in Australia, experts are warning.

image.altText: Tasmanian Devils will be released into a 1000 acre sanctuary to interact with other native mainland animals.

items.title: So much for the promise that Tasmanians deserve better from politicians

children.content: After last year's snap election, Tasmania's premier declared voters were "sick and tired of the political games". After this week, it is clear that the politicians just cannot help themselves.

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff leans to his left while seated.

avatar.alt: A woman with glasses stands in a TV studio.

items.title: How this bus stop that hasn't changed cost a city almost $9m

children.content: Repeated failed attempts to shift this bus stop has cost ratepayers millions over the past 15 years. The council is now trying again, but some warn it is already "a dog's breakfast".

gallery.altText: A bus arriving at a bus stop, a man with an assistance dog can be seen.

thumbnail.altText: A bus arriving at a bus stop, a man with an assistance dog can be seen.

items.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool may be drained for repairs after 'vandalism'

children.content: Donald Trump says the newly revamped Reflecting Pool needs "repairs" and that multiple people have been arrested for vandalising the Washington DC water feature.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool showing large patches of green
A man standing waist-deep in a green poll, holding a large cleaning tool

items.title: Why fuel excise cut is being extended by another month but at smaller discount

children.content: The fuel excise cut was due to stop at the end of the month but will continue to a lesser extent until August. Here's why.

image.altText: Two hands on a premium petrol pump.

items.title: Bird flu detection 'a distant concern' for Victoria, industry says

children.content: The head of Victoria's peak farming group says the detection of the deadly H5 variant of avian influenza in Western Australia is a "distant concern" for Victorians.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

images.altText: Daichi Kamada holds his hand up to his face like a phone in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Quick hits: Snakes at Germany's training and a player nicks a ref's watch

children.content: A Curaçao goalkeeper etches out a piece of World Cup history, Germany faces some slippery training pitch invaders and a referee's watch is briefly nicked by a player. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Quick Hits artwork June 21

articles.title: Is the most expensive World Cup in history worth the cost for travelling fans?

children.content: There has been endless talk about fans paying high prices for this World Cup. So is it worth it? And is it time to accept the people's game is no longer within everyone's reach?

image.altText: A composite image of several World Cup fans from different countries with face paint

children.content: Japan faces Tunisia in the 1000th game at a men's World Cup after Curaçao goalkeeper Eloy Room produces an inspired performance to deliver his nation its first-ever point. Follow live.

items.title: Japan leads Tunisia in milestone 1000th World Cup game

updates.title: Japan has four! Ueda has a brace!

items.title: James Tedesco in the wars as Roosters try to hold off Sharks

children.content: Referee Ashley Klein asks Roosters captain James Tedesco to clean some of the blood off his face before speaking to him during the second half of the Roosters' clash with the Sharks. Follow live.

image.altText: A bloodied James Tedesco speaks to referee Ashley Klein.

items.title: Bulldogs get the points at Docklands amid big injury worries for Saints

children.content: The Western Bulldogs get the points at Docklands against St Kilda to finish round 15, as the Saints lose two key players to injury.

image.altText: A Western Bulldogs tall forward is surrounded by teammates congratulating him after a vital goal.

items.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

image.altText: Players hug and smile as they celebrate in a huddle

items.title: They bought into Australia's tallest tower. Now their deposits are in limbo

children.content: Apartment buyers who were sold on ambitious plans for the tallest building in the country say they're facing up to a decade of uncertainty after the head-turning project faltered.

image.altText: a mock up image of what the tower would look like when complete.

items.title: How many clothes should you own? The maths behind a sustainable wardrobe

children.content: The environmental impact of a garment often depends on how many times it's worn. In a sense, every additional wear helps "offset" the garment's carbon footprint.

items.title: Love story: Coming out as a throuple

children.content: Everyone is bisexual, polyamorous, and has a crush on me. Or at least, that's my default assumption and the pick-up line that led to my triad with Rhea and Leo.

image.altText: An illustration of three people sitting together.

items.title: Is 'baby brain' real? A neuroscientist explains

children.content: The fog new parents feel is real, but it seems to be driven less by motherhood rewiring the brain than by exhaustion, overload and the relentless demands of caring for a tiny human.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman hugging and kissing a baby in a navy striped top

items.title: Protester tries unsuccessfully to stop voyage as whaling resumes in Iceland

children.content: A protester took an unplanned trip on an Icelandic whaling vessel, as the boat set out while he was in the crow's nest.

image.altText: A dead fin whale being hosed down.

items.title: Vet's chance discovery of first H5 bird flu variant case during beach walk

children.content: A walk along the beach with a friend turned into the significant discovery.

image.altText: a woman in a collard shirt and vest stands next to vetinary equipment

items.title: More missiles and ditch the UN: What One Nation’s foreign policy looks like

children.content: With One Nation running red hot in the polls and posing a serious challenge to the incumbent parties, there's more scrutiny on what its foreign policy would mean for Australia.

items.title: WA football great Cousins taking inspiration from Daniher in MND fight

children.content: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

image.altText: Close-up of a man with short grey hair

items.title: Police speech stuns descendants gathered at site of outback mass killing

children.content: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.

image.altText: a kimberly landscape aerial shows old buildings scattered in the scrub, with a winding river stretching through the frame

items.title: French soccer coach using Duolingo to help train his Aussie team

children.content: He may not know the language, but French-speaking William Loison is embracing his position coaching a regional Victorian soccer team with the help of a popular language app.

image.altText: A man holds a soccer ball

items.title: Did you pay attention at school? Let's see about that

children.content: Think you can get 10 out of 10 on our spelling quiz? Just remember, autocorrect can't save you here.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: War helped 're-enliven' Iran's regime. Trump's truce may complicate that

children.content: For decades the Islamic republic has been using its "main enemy" America to distract from its repressive regime, analysts say. But the recent truce with Donald Trump could change that.

image.altText: A poster depicting Donald Trump's mouth stitched shut and covered by tape shaped like the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Al Jazeera cameraman killed in Israeli strike in Gaza

children.content: Gaza health officials say Israeli strikes have killed at least 11 people, including two children and an Al Jazeera cameraman, in the latest violence to rock the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire.

image.altText: A man filing with a phone on a tripod, turning back to look at the camera

items.title: The quest for the truth behind Australian nurses' massacre in WWII

children.content: A relative of one of the 21 Australian nurses massacred by Japanese soldiers on a remote Indonesian beach in WWII wants the real story of what happened to be told.

items.title: The people bringing life back to Maryborough's heritage buildings

children.content: Some Maryborough residents are secretly restoring several heritage-listed buildings to their original state, transforming them in a bid to create an entertainment and dining precinct in  the regional Queensland city.

image.altText: A man with dark framed glasses smiles while leaning against a brick alcove.

items.title: 'New solutions': Modular homes set for NSW to help solve housing crisis

children.content: The modular home model would involve sections of rooms being constructed off-site in a dedicated facility before being transported to new developments across NSW.

image.altText: the outside of a modular home

items.title: When US troops arrived during World War II, Aussies were soon 'kicking the hell out of them'

children.content: The American war effort made Brisbane its stronghold in 1942, and thousands of attractive American soldiers were situated in Brisbane. That didn't sit so well with the Australians.

items.title: Surprise gift leaves Aboriginal war veteran 'pleased as bloody punch'

children.content: It took Wiradjuri man Victor Bartley 16 years to be recognised for his military service. Now, 55 years after he came home, a charity has tracked him down to offer him thanks.

image.altText: a man stands wrapped in a quilt

items.title: Why a trip to Asia might be a good idea now

children.content: The war in the Middle East has not stopped Australians travelling but their preferences may be changing.

children.content: A decade after the last coal truck rumbled out of Leigh Creek, Baljit Singh Bajwa put millions of dollars on the line to invest in the shrinking mining town.

items.title: Family of shark attack victim says swift response 'saved' mother's life

children.content: A Sydney shark attack victim has been hailed "a survivor" by her family in a letter read out at a community swim event.

image.altText: Surf lifesavers in uniform stand on the beach with boards while others swim in the water in the morning sun.

items.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity'

children.content: Italy's Giorgia Meloni has taken to social media to tell US President Donald Trump to focus on his own popularity following his claim that she begged for a photo together.

items.title: The adoption centre helping lab rats find their forever homes

children.content: A first-of-its kind adoption centre is aiming to find permanent homes for small animals used in scientific research.

items.title: Seeking equality, Afghan cricketers head to world cup they cannot play in

children.content: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.
